title: How a Recursive Function Unblocked 10+ Developers at Allianz
summary: Thousands of SAS scripts. A clunky viewer. Weeks of permission tickets. Then a tiny recursive Python script that walked the tree once and put every file in front of every developer in VS Code.

title: Half Stupid: Teaching Minecraft Agents to Survive From Scratch
summary: Four AI agents dropped into Minecraft with no instructions, no labels, and no pre-built knowledge. They learned to eat, developed personalities, and formed social dynamics. Then I hit the ceiling and decided to build the whole world from scratch in Rust.

title: NeoCru is dead. Here's what it was.
summary: I built an AI-powered recruitment tool that automatically evaluated candidates against job requirements. It worked. But B2B SaaS go-to-market as a solo founder is a different beast. So I shut it down.

title: Egyptians Built Pyramids in Decades. I Built a Cooler One with Python in a Few Hours.
summary: I wrote a Python script that generates a full pyramid complex inside Minecraft — central pyramid, walls, moat, entrance corridor, and interior halls. The whole thing took a few hours. The Egyptians needed 20 years.

title: We Migrated 600+ Tables to dbt. Here's How I Made Sure Nothing Broke.
summary: I built a dbt macro that automatically validates every migrated model against its original counterpart after every run, broken down by source, catching exact row-level discrepancies in seconds instead of hours.

title: How I Automated SAS-to-Snowflake Column Matching with AI
summary: We had hundreds of tables on both SAS and Snowflake with different column names, different content, and extra columns on both sides. I built a pipeline that reduced per-table matching from days to minutes.
